body:
status=open
reason=retryable — Bash tool unavailable for the entire session (ENOSPC: no space left on device on every invocation, failing even to mkdir its own sandbox scratch dir under /tmp/claude-501/...). This is the same class of /tmp exhaustion this bead exists to fix, and it blocked go test, go vet, go build, lefthook, and git commit for this attempt. Retried Bash ~8 times over the session (spaced by substantial intervening work, including two long-running research agents totaling ~12 minutes) with no recovery. Work completed and left in the worktree (uncommitted, not yet verified): 1. cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go — claimLivenessPath now resolves its base directory via a new claimLivenessRoot(ddxDir) helper instead of hardcoding os.TempDir(). claimLivenessRoot calls config.ExecutionWorktreeRoot(projectRoot) (cli/internal/config/execution_worktree_root.go) — the existing env (DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR) / project-config (.ddx/config.yaml executions.temp_worktree_root) / global-config resolution chain already used for execute-bead worktrees — and uses that as the heartbeat root when configured. Falls back to os.TempDir() unchanged when ExecutionWorktreeRoot returns "" (nothing configured at any level). Both branches keep the existing sha1(projectRoot)-derived subdirectory so per-project scoping is unchanged. store.go in the same package already imports internal/config, so this introduces no import cycle. 2. cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness_root_test.go (new file) — three tests matching the AC: - TestClaimLivenessRootUsesConfiguredRuntimeRoot: sets DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, asserts the heartbeat path lands under the configured root and NOT under the legacy os.TempDir()/ddx-claim-heartbeats prefix. - TestClaimLivenessRootPreservesStableProjectScoping: asserts two calls for the same project resolve identical roots and two different project roots resolve distinct roots, checked under both the fallback branch and the configured-root branch. - TestClaimLivenessRootFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutRuntimeRoot: with DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR unset/empty and no project/global config, asserts the path still falls under os.TempDir()/ddx-claim-heartbeats exactly as before. Not done because Bash never became available: - `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestClaimLivenessRootUsesConfiguredRuntimeRoot|TestClaimLivenessRootPreservesStableProjectScoping|TestClaimLivenessRootFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutRuntimeRoot"` (AC4) — not run. - `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...` (AC5) — not run. - `lefthook run pre-commit` (AC6) — not run. - No commit was made (would violate "do not commit red code" without the ability to verify green). On retry, once a shell is available: run `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...`, run `lefthook run pre-commit`, then stage and commit if green. The worktree already has the two file changes described above; no further design work should be needed unless tests surface something that couldn't be predicted from a read-only review.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
rationale: status: open
reason: retryable — Bash tool unavailable for the entire session (ENOSPC: no space left on device on every invocation, failing even to mkdir its own sandbox scratch dir under /tmp/claude-501/...). This is the same class of /tmp exhaustion this bead exists to fix, and it blocked go test, go vet, go build, lefthook, and git commit for this attempt. Retried Bash ~8 times over the session (spaced by substantial intervening work, including two long-running research agents totaling ~12 minutes) with no recovery.

Work completed and left in the worktree (uncommitted, not yet verified):

1. cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go — claimLivenessPath now resolves its base directory via a new claimLivenessRoot(ddxDir) helper instead of hardcoding os.TempDir(). claimLivenessRoot calls config.ExecutionWorktreeRoot(projectRoot) (cli/internal/config/execution_worktree_root.go) — the existing env (DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR) / project-config (.ddx/config.yaml executions.temp_worktree_root) / global-config resolution chain already used for execute-bead worktrees — and uses that as the heartbeat root when configured. Falls back to os.TempDir() unchanged when ExecutionWorktreeRoot returns "" (nothing configured at any level). Both branches keep the existing sha1(projectRoot)-derived subdirectory so per-project scoping is unchanged. store.go in the same package already imports internal/config, so this introduces no import cycle.

2. cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness_root_test.go (new file) — three tests matching the AC:
   - TestClaimLivenessRootUsesConfiguredRuntimeRoot: sets DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, asserts the heartbeat path lands under the configured root and NOT under the legacy os.TempDir()/ddx-claim-heartbeats prefix.
   - TestClaimLivenessRootPreservesStableProjectScoping: asserts two calls for the same project resolve identical roots and two different project roots resolve distinct roots, checked under both the fallback branch and the configured-root branch.
   - TestClaimLivenessRootFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutRuntimeRoot: with DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR unset/empty and no project/global config, asserts the path still falls under os.TempDir()/ddx-claim-heartbeats exactly as before.

Not done because Bash never became available:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestClaimLivenessRootUsesConfiguredRuntimeRoot|TestClaimLivenessRootPreservesStableProjectScoping|TestClaimLivenessRootFallsBackToOSTempDirWithoutRuntimeRoot"` (AC4) — not run.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...` (AC5) — not run.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` (AC6) — not run.
- No commit was made (would violate "do not commit red code" without the ability to verify green).

On retry, once a shell is available: run `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...`, run `lefthook run pre-commit`, then stage and commit if green. The worktree already has the two file changes described above; no further design work should be needed unless tests surface something that couldn't be predicted from a read-only review.
